entries.title: ON THE FIRST BOOK OF KINGS 20:1 on first-kings 20:20
entries.excerpt: Here the Scripture relates the two battles of the king of Israel against the Arameans, at which we have already hinted, and the twin slaughters of the Arameans, of which the second caused the death of 127, men, as God took his revenge on the impious voice of the Arameans, who said about the true God worshiped by the Israelites, "The Lord is a god of the hills, but he is not a god of the valleys."

entries.title: THE GOVERNANCE OF GOD 7.8 on first-kings 20:20
entries.excerpt: Did not the Lord wish Ben-hadad, king of Syria, whom besides countless thousands of his own people, thirty-two kings and armies of the same number of kings served, to be conquered by a few foot soldiers of the princes in order that he who was the author of such victory would be acknowledged?
